key_insight: A coupled LSTM + physics-informed GNN can reconstruct history-dependent, spatially varying stress fields in nonlinear microstructures by separating time-path encoding from equilibrium-constrained spatial prediction.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast surrogate stress-field prediction for elasto-plastic multiscale simulation, offering orders-of-magnitude speedup while remaining transferable across mesh resolutions and element types.
method: LSTM encodes macroscopic stress-strain loading history into a compact latent state.
Physics-informed GNN/MeshGraphNet reconstructs local stress on the mesh using connectivity and a discrete divergence equilibrium penalty.
A linear warm-up weighting balances reconstruction loss and physics loss to stabilize training in the elasto-plastic regime.
evidence_signal: Trained on 10,000 non-proportional loading paths for a periodic plate-with-a-hole microstructure, the model reportedly achieves about 1000× speedup over finite elements and generalizes to sequences twice as long with 1.9% cumulative error.
watch_item: Transferability depends on having a mesh/connectivity representation consistent with the trained physics and loading regime, so robustness outside the demonstrated constitutive setting may require retraining or further validation.
paper_title: Non-linear mechanical field reconstruction coupling recurrent neural networks with physics-informed graph neural networks
